stem: A hospital is considering a mesh topology for a critical monitoring network.
model_answer: In a mesh topology each node is connected to many other nodes, usually with multiple paths between any two devices. Data can take a different route if a link or node fails, which makes the network very fault tolerant and reliable - important for a hospital where downtime could harm patients. The main drawbacks are cost and complexity: a full mesh needs many more cables and network interfaces than a star, and configuring/managing the routing is harder.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain one advantage and one disadvantage of using a mesh topology in this hospital network.

mark_points.text: advantage: multiple paths exist between devices
accepted_alternatives: each node connects to several others

mark_points.text: advantage developed: so if one link or device fails, data can be re-routed and the network keeps working (high reliability)
accepted_alternatives: fault tolerant - no single point of failure
critical monitoring stays online if a cable breaks

mark_points.text: disadvantage: expensive / requires lots of cabling and network interfaces
disadvantage developed: complex to set up and maintain / routing is harder to configure
accepted_alternatives: difficult to manage as network grows
